title: Pie Chart: The chart below shows the percentage of the day working adults spent doing diffe...
description: The chart below shows the percentage of the day working adults spent doing different activities in a particular country in 1958 and in 2008.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Pie charts: Percentage of day spent on different activities (1958 vs 2008)

1. Giải thích đề

Hai biểu đồ tròn cho thấy tỷ lệ thời gian trong ngày được dành cho các hoạt động khác nhau vào năm 1958 và 2008.

→ Dạng: pie charts – so sánh 2 mốc thời gian

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ các từ khóa:

percentage of day

different activities

two years

Có thể dùng:

shows → illustrates / compares

time spent → time allocated

Mẫu:

The pie charts illustrate how people allocated their time to different activities in 1958 and 2008.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Working chiếm tỷ lệ lớn nhất ở cả hai năm và tăng đáng kể

Sleeping giảm

Travelling to work tăng rõ

Các hoạt động thư giãn tại nhà giảm

→ xu hướng: cuộc sống hiện đại dành nhiều thời gian cho công việc và di chuyển hơn

Từ khóa nên dùng:

accounted for the largest share

increased significantly

declined

showed notable changes

4. Chia BODY

BODY 1 – Nhóm chính (Working, Sleeping, Travelling)

Working:

33% → 42%

→ tăng mạnh, lớn nhất

Sleeping:

32% → 25%

→ giảm đáng kể

Travelling to work:

19% → 13% (thực tế giảm nhẹ, cần chú ý)

Ý chính: thời gian làm việc tăng trong khi ngủ giảm

BODY 2 – Các hoạt động còn lại

Relaxing at home:

8% → 6% (giảm nhẹ)

Going out:

6% → 6% (không đổi)

Other (hobbies/sport):

2% → 8% (tăng rõ)

Ý chính: các hoạt động giải trí thay đổi nhẹ, nhưng hobbies tăng lên đáng kể

5. Gợi ý từ vựng

account for

make up

increase significantly

decline moderately

remain unchanged

show a slight change

Ví dụ:

Working accounted for the largest proportion in both years and increased significantly.

The percentage of time spent sleeping declined noticeably.

Travelling to work showed a slight decrease.

Time spent on hobbies and sports increased considerably.

The two pie charts compare how people allocated their daily time across six activities in 1958 and 2008.

Overall, working and sleeping accounted for the largest shares in both years, although the proportion of time spent working increased markedly, while sleeping and commuting declined. Meanwhile, leisure-related activities showed relatively minor changes.

In 1958, working occupied the largest proportion of the day at 33%, closely followed by sleeping at 32%. Travelling to work made up 19%, while relaxing at home accounted for 8%. Smaller proportions were devoted to going out (6%) and other activities such as hobbies or sport (2%).

By 2008, the proportion of time spent working had risen significantly to 42%, making it by far the dominant activity. In contrast, sleeping decreased to 25%, and commuting fell to 13%. Relaxing at home declined slightly to 6%, while the figure for going out remained unchanged at 6%. Notably, time spent on other activities increased fourfold to 8%.
